cover.hook: Fable 5 is back — the 19-day AI model ban on Canadian users is over.
cover.sub: Plus $39M for Northern Ontario communities, 74 active wildfires, a July 31 funding deadline, and Canada's biggest privacy law in 25 years.

reelSub: Fable 5 returns, $39M for Northern Ontario, and 74 fires across the Northwest.
storySub: Five moves from this week that affect how you build, fund, and run an NWO business.

items.title: Fable 5 ban lifted — Canadian users have access again
items.body: US export controls on Fable 5 were lifted June 30; Anthropic restored global access July 1. A new safety classifier blocks the jailbreak that triggered the shutdown. The framework enabling a repeat is now more formalized.
items.tease: Next: $39M flowing into Northern Ontario communities.
items.reelLine: Fable 5 ban lifted July 1 — Canadian AI users are back online.

items.title: FedNor opens $39M for Northern Ontario community infrastructure
items.body: Municipalities and Indigenous organizations across Northern Ontario can now apply to repair and upgrade community facilities through the federal Build Communities Strong Fund. Announced in Thunder Bay June 30.
items.tease: Next: the fire count that jumped from 13 to 74.
items.reelLine: $39M federal stream open now for Northern Ontario communities.

items.title: 74 active wildfires across the Northwest — 17 uncontrolled
items.body: The Northwest Fire Region had 74 active fires as of July 4, up from 13 on June 25. Three large complexes are driving resources. A drying trend is expected to worsen conditions.
items.tease: Next: a funding deadline 25 days away.
items.reelLine: 74 wildfires burning in the Northwest — 17 uncontrolled as of July 4.

items.title: NORDS Fund closes July 31 — $400K available per Northern Ontario municipality
items.body: All 144 Northern Ontario municipalities can apply for up to $400,000 in infrastructure grants through the provincial NORDS Fund before July 31 at 5 PM EDT. Up to 100% of eligible capital costs covered.
items.tease: Next: Canada's biggest privacy law in 25 years.
items.reelLine: NORDS Fund deadline: July 31. Up to $400K per NWO municipality.

items.title: Canada tables its first AI-era privacy law — Bill C-36
items.body: Bill C-36 requires businesses to explain automated decision-making, honour data-deletion requests, and disclose AI use affecting individuals. Not yet law; fines up to 5% of global revenue proposed.
items.reelLine: Bill C-36: Canada's first AI-era privacy law is before Parliament.

summary.title: AI access restored, public money flowing north, and new rules on the horizon
summary.body: This week the Fable 5 ban lifted and Canadian AI access is restored, $39M in federal community funding opened for Northern Ontario, a key provincial infrastructure deadline arrives July 31, and Canada's first AI-era privacy law started its path through Parliament.

captions.carousel: Fable 5 is back for Canadian users after a 19-day ban. FedNor opened a $39M community infrastructure stream for Northern Ontario municipalities and Indigenous organizations. Wildfire conditions across the Northwest jumped from 13 to 74 active fires. The provincial NORDS Fund deadline is July 31 — up to $400K per municipality. And Canada's biggest privacy reform in 25 years is now before Parliament. Full breakdown in The Signal, link in bio. #ThunderBay #NorthwesternOntario #NorthernOntario #AIbusiness #FedNor #NWOntario
captions.reel: Fable 5 back online. $39M for Northern Ontario. 74 wildfires. July 31 funding deadline. Canada's AI-era privacy law. Five moves from this week. thunderbayai.com #NorthernOntario #ThunderBay #AIbusiness #FedNor
